headline: OpenCode billing rejection surfaced as unknown runtime error
hypothesis: OpenCode nested APIError payloads placed the actionable provider message under error.data.message, which the adapter did not parse; once surfaced, insufficient-balance wording was not classified as a quota-like pre-work failure.

blurb_segments.text: The failing OpenCode path was a nonzero structured provider API error before any tool work.
The adapter previously missed error.data.message and fell back to raw JSON diagnostics.
The classifier did not recognize insufficient-balance wording as failover-eligible quota exhaustion.

c1.note: The QA finding came from the OpenCode runtime logger and contained a structured provider APIError with an insufficient-balance message.

c2.note: The previous payload renderer inspected message/detail/error/code but not nested data payloads.

c3.note: The prior failover marker list included quota and usage-limit wording but not billing balance or credit exhaustion.

evidence_lines.msg: OpenCode CLI exited with code 1: structured APIError stdout contained an insufficient-balance provider message.
Adapter structured error selection did not recurse into the APIError data object.
Failover classifier markers covered quota exceeded and usage limit but not insufficient balance.

counter_evidence.hypothesis: This is only an external provider billing issue and needs no code change.
counter_evidence.reason: The provider account state is external, but the runtime should still surface the actionable nested message and classify the pre-work rejection for same-tier failover.

counter_evidence.hypothesis: The adapter executed tools before the failure, so failover would be unsafe.
counter_evidence.reason: The failing path is a nonzero CLI provider rejection and the adapter records it as pre-tool-call.

why_this_fix: Parsing error.data.message preserves the actionable provider detail. Adding balance and credit markers lets the failover classifier handle the resulting RuntimeError as quota-like pre-work rejection.

diff_snapshot.text: Regression coverage for nested APIError data.message extraction.
Regression coverage for classifying OpenCode insufficient-balance failures.
